rows.notes: Consensus across all extractors; visually verified.

Pounds, shillings, and whole pence values were agreed upon by both extractors. For the fraction, `gpt-5-mini`'s note acknowledged the visual ambiguity more accurately than `gemini-flash`'s 'clearly visible ¾'. Visual inspection suggests the fraction mark is a single stroke, which is more consistent with 0.25 (¼) or 0.5 (½) than 0.75 (¾). The chosen value reflects the most plausible visual interpretation for this line, supported by `gpt-5-mini`'s reasoning.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Various payments brought forward, amounting to £999 2s 9d.

rows.description: Interest to the library Fund. on 3060.14.11 et &c. to Mich. 1829 - one year
rows.notes: The description was adapted from `gemini-flash` as 'et &c.' is clearly visible in the image, unlike `gpt-5-mini`'s 'at 4 pr Ct'. The pounds amount '122' was selected from `gpt-5-mini` and visually verified; `gemini-flash`'s '312' is incorrect. Shillings, pence, and fraction are clear and were agreed upon by both extractors.

rows.description: To Mr Harrington. for a journey to Bampton with Mr Davenport the Surveyor
rows.notes: The description was chosen based on `gemini-flash` for the correct period after 'Harrington'. Amounts were selected based on `gpt-5-mini`'s reading and detailed visual verification; `gpt-5-mini` correctly identified the leading '3' as a separate reference mark, not part of the amount. The actual amounts are clearly '2' pounds, '3' shillings, and '0' pence. `gemini-flash`'s reading of '3' pounds, '2' shillings, '3' pence is incorrect.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Harrington for travel to Bampton with the Surveyor Mr Davenport.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Harrington for travel to Bampton with the Surveyor Mr Davenport, amounting to £2 3s 0d.

The description is a combination: it includes the leading dash (from `gpt-5-mini`) and 'Grove' (from `gemini-flash`), as both are clearly visible in the image.
